symbols.name: AssumeUtc(DateTime)
symbols.summary: Returns a UTC System.DateTime for values that are already UTC or have no kind.
symbols.remarks: Local values are rejected to avoid hiding a machine-local conversion. var stored = new DateTime(2026, 1, 1); var utc = stored.AssumeUtc();

syntax.content: public static DateTime AssumeUtc(this DateTime source)

parameters.description: The source value to treat as UTC.

returns.description: A UTC System.DateTime with the same clock value.

parameters.description: The source value to treat as UTC.

returns.description: A UTC System.DateTime with the same clock value.

symbols.name: EnsureUtc(DateTime)
symbols.summary: Ensures a System.DateTime is usable as UTC.
symbols.remarks: Unspecified values are interpreted as UTC. Local values are rejected. var utc = value.EnsureUtc();

syntax.content: public static DateTime EnsureUtc(this DateTime source)

returns.description: A UTC System.DateTime with the same clock value.
A UTC System.DateTime with the same clock value.

symbols.summary: Determines whether a value has System.DateTimeKind.Utc.

syntax.content: public static bool IsUtc(this DateTime source)

returns.description: true when the value is UTC; otherwise false.
true when the value is UTC; otherwise false.

syntax.content: public static DateTime? ParseDateOrEpoch(this string source)

symbols.name: ParseDateOrEpoch(string, AmbiguousDatePolicy)
symbols.summary: Parses a date string or Unix epoch value into a UTC System.DateTime.
symbols.remarks: Epoch seconds and milliseconds return UTC. Date strings without offsets are interpreted as UTC. var parsed = "1735689600000".ParseDateOrEpoch();

syntax.content: public static DateTime? ParseDateOrEpoch(this string source, AmbiguousDatePolicy ambiguousDatePolicy)

parameters.description: The policy used for ambiguous slash dates.

returns.description: A UTC System.DateTime when parsing succeeds; otherwise null.

parameters.description: The policy used for ambiguous slash dates.

returns.description: A UTC System.DateTime when parsing succeeds; otherwise null.

symbols.name: ParseDateOrEpochOrThrow(string)
symbols.summary: Parses a date string or Unix epoch value into a UTC System.DateTime or throws.
symbols.remarks: Ambiguous slash dates are rejected by default. var parsed = "2026-01-01".ParseDateOrEpochOrThrow();

syntax.content: public static DateTime ParseDateOrEpochOrThrow(this string source)

returns.description: The parsed UTC value.
The parsed UTC value.

symbols.name: TryParseDateOrEpoch(string, out DateTime, AmbiguousDatePolicy)
symbols.summary: Attempts to parse a date string or Unix epoch value into a UTC System.DateTime.
symbols.remarks: Epoch milliseconds are detected by 13 or more digits. Slash dates such as 03/04/2026 are rejected unless a policy is provided. if ("1735689600".TryParseDateOrEpoch(out var parsed)) { }

syntax.content: public static bool TryParseDateOrEpoch(this string source, out DateTime result, AmbiguousDatePolicy ambiguousDatePolicy)

parameters.description: The parsed UTC value when parsing succeeds.
The policy used for ambiguous slash dates.

returns.description: true when parsing succeeds; otherwise false.

The policy used for ambiguous slash dates.

returns.description: true when parsing succeeds; otherwise false.

symbols.summary: Determines whether a given date is within a specified relative range from the current date.

The amount of the unit to define the range.
The direction relative to the current date (Past or Future).
Specifies whether the range should be inclusive of the boundary dates.

returns.description: True if the date is within the specified relative range; otherwise, false.

returns.description: true when the date is inside the range; otherwise false.
